hero.subtitle: Explore real-time climate intelligence from 60+ cities across 35 countries — visualize the path to net-zero, learn from leading initiatives, and join the global community shaping a sustainable future.

how.heading: How it works
how.sub: Three pillars connect insight to action: see the world, learn the system, drive change.

step1.title: Discover
step1.body: Browse 60+ cities on the interactive map. Filter by carbon tier, surface key initiatives, see who's leading the transition.

step2.title: Understand
step2.body: Navigate concept maps that connect technologies, sectors, and policies. Bookmark resources, ask the AI assistant.

step3.title: Act
step3.body: Build city scenarios in the simulator. Share findings with the community. Submit research as an expert.

featured.heading: Cities leading the transition
featured.sub: A small sample from the 60+ cities mapped across 35 countries.

quote.text: We won't decarbonize the planet one country at a time. We'll do it one city at a time — and we need every operator, every researcher, every citizen to see the same map.
quote.name: Dr. Amani Al-Saadi
quote.role: Climate Policy Lead, MENA Climate Initiative

framework.title: What is the Circular Carbon Economy?
framework.subtitle: A comprehensive framework built on four core pillars for sustainable carbon emissions management

reduce.sub: Reducing emissions at the source through energy efficiency optimization and clean industrial transformation
reduce.body: The foundation of the CCE model focuses on minimizing carbon output through energy-efficient infrastructure, cleaner industrial processes, and smarter resource management across all sectors of the economy.

reuse.sub: Repurposing carbon compounds as raw materials for new industrial and biological production cycles
reuse.body: Rather than treating carbon as a waste product, the reuse pillar transforms captured carbon into valuable feedstocks for manufacturing, chemical production, and the creation of sustainable synthetic fuels.

recycle.sub: Recycling carbon back into organic systems through biological and geological carbon cycling processes
recycle.body: This pillar leverages natural carbon cycles and biotech innovations to reintegrate carbon into ecosystems, supporting soil carbon sequestration, biochar production, and bio-based material manufacturing.

remove.sub: Permanently removing atmospheric CO₂ through direct air capture and geological storage technologies
remove.body: The removal tier addresses legacy carbon through cutting-edge technologies including enhanced weathering, direct air capture (DAC), and deep geological storage — targeting net-negative emissions pathways by 2050.

app.title: Knowledge in your hands, anytime
app.subtitle: Download the app to access knowledge content and engage with the community from anywhere.
